title: Burning
excerpt: Burning is an ongoing fire-harm state in which a creature, object, or surface remains actively aflame and can continue dealing damage or spreading danger until something ends the ignition.
markdown:
# Burning

Burning is the state of an actively ignited creature, object, or surface that continues to carry fire through the world instead of resolving as a single completed flash of harm. While something is burning, the fire can keep threatening [[hit_points]], space control, equipment, or nearby materials until the ignition is ended.

## Rules Use

### Ongoing Fire State
- Burning means the fire remains present after the initial ignition event
- The source that set the target ablaze determines how long the burning persists, how it spreads, and when further [[damage]] is applied
- If no more specific rule says otherwise, treat burning as a continuing hazard rather than as a one-moment burst

### Burning Is Not Automatically A Named Condition
- Burning can exist as a meaningful world-state even when no formal condition entry has been applied
- Another effect can still add conditions, movement limits, or panic outcomes on top of the burning state
- The fire's rules text determines whether the target is merely taking recurring harm or also suffering other restrictions

### Repeated Harm And Disruption
- Burning most often deals fire harm, which should resolve through the relevant entry in [[damage_types|Damage Types]] when type-based defenses or weaknesses matter
- Repeated burning damage can break [[concentration]] the same way any other qualifying damage can
- A burning target keeps carrying risk until the flames are extinguished, the source expires, or the target is otherwise no longer able to burn in the same way

## Resolution Meaning

Burning matters because the hazard remains live in the world-state. The important question is not only how much damage the ignition dealt at first, but whether fire is still present and still entitled to change what happens next.
